title: Briny Saudi-Arabian Masarepa Fritters
description: Crunchy fritters made with white masarepa, onions, and herbs, perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine masarepa, onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Mix well with a fork until the ingredients are evenly distributed.
Gradually add water to the mixture and stir until a thick dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and pliable.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Using a cookie scoop or your hands, shape the dough into small balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the disks to the skillet (do not overcrowd).
Cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until the fritters are golden brown and crispy.
Repeat with the remaining dough, adjusting the heat as needed to prevent burning.
Serve the fritters hot with chopped parsley and cilantro on top.
